title: Corallodiscus bhutanicus
what_description: Corallodiscus bhutanicus is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae. It is an epiphytic perennial herb with a woody rhizome, producing several erect stems that are 15-40 cm tall and 2-5 cm in diameter. The leaves are opposite, ovate to elliptic in shape, 10-20 cm long and 4-8 cm wide, with a leathery texture, entire margin and a distinctive red or purple midrib. The inflorescence is a terminal spike bearing 10-20 tubular flowers, 3-5 cm long, with a pink to purple color and a white or yellow center. The flowers are bilaterally symmetrical, with a long tube and four petal-like lobes.

properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Corallodiscus bhutanicus are its antioxidant capabilities, which protect cells from oxidative damage and potentially reduce the risk of chronic diseases. The plant's bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, are responsible for its antimicrobial properties, inhibiting the growth of various microorganisms and preventing infections. The anti-inflammatory properties of Corallodiscus bhutanicus are attributed to its saponin content, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate conditions such as arthritis. Additionally, the cardioprotective effects of the plant are associated with its ability to lower lipid peroxidation and improve cardiovascular health. Furthermore, the analgesic properties of Corallodiscus bhutanicus are thought to be mediated by its volatile oil and saponin content, providing relief from pain and discomfort.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Corallodiscus bhutanicus are flavonoids such as quercetin and kaempferol which exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. Triterpenoids like corosolic acid and ursolic acid have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antineoplastic properties. Iridoid glycosides like coralloid and corallidin have shown to possess antiviral and antibacterial activities. Phenolic acids such as caffeic acid and ferulic acid have been found to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. Saponins like corallodiscoside have been found to possess hepatoprotective and anti-inflammatory properties.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Corallodiscus bhutanicus are tea, infusion, tincture, decoction, and salve. A tea is prepared by steeping the flowers or leaves of Corallodiscus bhutanicus in hot water, while an infusion involves steeping the plant material in cold water for a longer period. A tincture is a concentrated liquid extract of the plant, typically made with a solvent such as ethanol or glycerin, and is often used for its medicinal properties. Decoction is a preparation method where the plant material is boiled in water to release its active compounds, and Corallodiscus bhutanicus decoction is used to treat various ailments, including fever and inflammation. A salve is a topical preparation made from the plant's leaves or flowers, and is used to treat skin conditions such as wounds and burns.

taxonomy: Corallodiscus bhutanicus is a species of plant in the family Gesneriaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the African violet family. The genus Corallodiscus is a group of flowering plants native to Asia. This plant is classified as a member of the order Lamiales, which includes a wide range of flowering plants such as mint, lavender, and honeysuckle.
